alerts.title: Iran uranium enrichment reaches 60% U-235; breakout window narrows to 1-2 weeks
alerts.summary: Global metrics nuclear headlines confirm Iran has achieved 60% uranium enrichment with estimated breakout time of 1-2 weeks (global_metrics, June 12). IAEA board passed resolution demanding Iran declare uranium stockpiles and grant inspectors access. France and multiple governments warn Iran has continuously violated NPT commitments. This represents material acceleration toward weapons-grade material.

alerts.title: Iran-Israel ceasefire collapses; Trump calls off strike threats amid conflicting deal narratives
alerts.summary: Incidents feed reports contradictory messaging June 12: Trump announced calling off threatened strikes citing breakthrough in US-Iran talks (PBS, AP News), while simultaneously denying Iran's account of deal terms and calling Iranians 'dishonorable' (CNBC). Pakistani PM claims final peace deal text reached (RFE/RL). Meanwhile, Israeli officials state Iran's 14-point proposal demands $300B 'recovery plan' and complete US troop withdrawal while leaving missile program off negotiation table (Media Line). Diplomatic chaos signals framework instability despite public de-escalation rhetoric.

alerts.title: Indian sailors killed in Hormuz as ceasefire framework fails for third-party maritime traffic
alerts.summary: Multiple outlets (Al Jazeera, CNN, June 12) report Indian sailors becoming 'collateral damage' in Trump's Iran war, with vessels attacked in Strait of Hormuz despite supposed ceasefire. Highlights gap between US-Iran diplomatic pause and ongoing proxy/asymmetric threats to neutral shipping. Critical infrastructure vulnerability persists even if kinetic US-Iran exchanges pause.

alerts.title: Iran-linked cyber groups claim FBI drone system theft and California water infrastructure compromise
alerts.summary: Global metrics cyber headlines report Iran-linked groups claiming hacks of FBI drone systems and California water systems in direct retaliation for Iran strikes (CBS News, Middle East Eye, June 12). Claims represent escalation into critical infrastructure targeting and asymmetric response during kinetic pause. Threat level maintained at 9/10 (CRITICAL—IMMINENT ESCALATION) per global_metrics.

alerts.title: US maintains ~30 warships in Middle East theater despite diplomatic talks; carriers remain positioned for rapid escalation
alerts.summary: Assets feed confirms USS Dwight D. Eisenhower, USS Abraham Lincoln, and USS Gerald R. Ford deployed across Red Sea, Arabian Sea, and Mediterranean with multiple destroyer and submarine support (Carney, Mason, Laboon in Red Sea; Florida in Persian Gulf). Fleet composition unchanged despite Trump's announced de-escalation, signaling contingency readiness. Rybar Telegram post (~88K views, June 12, 14:31 UTC) notes American naval grouping 'has not changed position' despite diplomatic theater.
alerts.region: Red Sea, Arabian Sea, Persian Gulf, Mediterranean

analysis_note: Iran's nuclear program has accelerated to weapons-adjacent thresholds (60% enrichment, 1-2 week breakout) while US-Iran diplomatic framework collapses into competing narratives; kinetic pauses mask persistent proxy escalation, cyber retaliation, and third-party casualty risks as Trump administration maintains full military positioning.
